data.title: Tether halts secondary sales amid talks to secure $20B funding for $500B valuation
data.description:
Stablecoin issuer Tether has stopped secondary share sales while pushing ahead with talks to raise as much as $20 billion in a stock deal tied to a $500 billion valuation, according to Bloomberg. The decision was reportedly made in order to block attempts by existing shareholders to sell stakes outside the company’s process, a step […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/tether-halts-secondary-sales/

data.title: Two FOMC voting members explained why they opposed interest rate cuts: Inflation risks are too high, and greater caution is needed.
data.description: Chicago Fed President John Goolsby said his dissenting vote this week was his first since joining the Fed in 2023, putting him on the same side as Kansas City Fed President Schmid. He voted against the tariffs because he wanted to wait for more economic data to determine whether their impact on inflation was temporary. In a statement on Friday, Goolsby said, “Given that inflation has been above our target for four and a half years, and that progress on inflation has stalled for months, coupled with the fact that almost all businesses and consumers we’ve spoken to in our district recently have listed prices as a major concern, I felt it was more prudent to wait for more information.” (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Hamak: The Federal Reserve is investing a lot of time in researching artificial intelligence.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Hamak stated that the Fed is investing significant time in researching artificial intelligence and is confident that the next Fed chair will focus on the 2% inflation target. (Jinshi)

data.title: How Are Ratings Agencies Adapting to the Rise of Digital Assets
data.description:
Credit rating agencies are building new frameworks for crypto and tokenized assets, but the shift won’t immediately alter traditional credit scores as regulators in the US, Europe, and Asia race to impose clarity on a fast-moving market.
source: https://www.blockhead.co/2025/12/12/how-are-ratings-agencies-adapting-to-the-rise-of-digital-assets/

data.description:
Despite fresh institutional adoption, newly launched XRP exchange traded funds (ETFs), and growing cross chain experimentation, XRP has quietly lost … Continue reading The post ChatGPT 5.
source: https://finbold.com/chatgpt-5-2-predicts-xrp-price-for-the-end-of-2025/

data.title: Federal Reserve's Goolsby: If reserves are insufficient, it's impossible to set interest rates at the desired level.
data.description: Federal Reserve's Goolsby stated that without sufficient reserves, it's impossible to set interest rates at the desired level; with ample reserves, the balance sheet must expand as the economy grows. Furthermore, Federal Reserve's Hamak stated that an independent central bank yields better results, and the lack of data makes interpreting inflation data more difficult. He found some comfort in market-based inflation indicators, a source of optimism regarding the path of price increases, and expressed "great gratitude" for the return of government data, noting the abundance of alternative data in the labor market. (Jinshi)

data.title: After three years of silence, a whale deposited 200 BTC, worth $18.46 million, into Binance.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited 200 BTC, worth $18.46 million, into Binance after three years of inactivity. The whale initially withdrew 400 BTC, worth $9.83 million, from OKX. If it were to sell them now, it would make a profit of $27.03 million.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Schmid: Labor Market Cooling, Inflation Remains High
data.description: According to Odaily, Federal Reserve's Schmid has indicated that the labor market is cooling but remains largely balanced. Inflation continues to be high, and the economy is showing growth momentum. Since the Federal Reserve meeting in October, there have been no significant changes in the situation.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Goolsby: Not "hawkish" on next year's interest rate stance.
data.description: Federal Reserve's Goolsby stated that he is one of the most optimistic figures in the dot plot, not "hawkish" on interest rates next year, optimistic about a rate decline this year, but uneasy about premature and significant policy easing. (Golden Ten)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Goolsby: Expects more rate cuts than the median in 2026.
data.description: Federal Reserve's Goolsby stated, "I expect more rate cuts than the median in 2026, with uncertainty surrounding low hiring and low layoffs outweighing the economic slowdown. Changes in data such as monthly payrolls are making tasks like assessing break-even job creation rates difficult." (Jinshi)

data.title: Two newly created wallets withdrew 300,000 USDT from the CEX and have already deposited 200,000 USDT to buy 958,526 RAVE.
data.description: According to Lookonchian's monitoring, two newly created wallets (0x98DF and 0xEb2c) have just withdrawn 300,000 USDT from Bybit to purchase RAVE. They have already spent 200,000 USDT to purchase 958,526 RAVE, and may continue to use the remaining funds to purchase more.

data.title: USDC Treasury Burns Over 78 Million USDC on Ethereum
data.description: According to PANews, Whale Alert has reported that the USDC Treasury recently destroyed 78,904,497 USDC on the Ethereum blockchain. This transaction is valued at approximately $78.9 million.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Schmid: Inflation has not changed much since the October Fed meeting.
data.description: Federal Reserve official Schmid stated that the labor market is cooling but remains largely balanced, inflation remains too high, and the economy is showing growth momentum; the situation has not changed significantly since the Fed meeting in October. (Jinshi)

data.title: Barclays lowered its price target for Coinbase stock to $291.
data.description: Barclays lowered its price target for Coinbase to $291 from $357, but maintained a "neutral" rating. In a broader update to its outlook for brokers, asset managers, and exchanges in 2026, Barclays noted a favorable market environment, particularly for alternative asset managers and wealth brokers; however, the future environment for exchanges and traditional asset managers will be more complex.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Schmid: A moderately restrictive monetary policy should be maintained.
data.description: Kansas City Federal Reserve President Schmid stated that he opposes the Fed's 25-basis-point rate cut this week, arguing that inflation "remains too high" and monetary policy should remain moderately restrictive to curb price pressures. Schmid said, "What I see right now is an economy that still has momentum, and inflation is 'too hot,' which suggests that current policy is not overly tight." Schmid pointed out that since he opposed the rate cut in October, the situation "hasn't changed much": inflation remains above the 2% target, the labor market is "generally balanced," and there is no urgent reason for a rate cut. (Jinshi)

data.title: Chicago Fed President Opposes Recent Fed Rate Cut Decision
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee expressed opposition to the Federal Reserve's recent decision to cut interest rates by 25 basis points. He emphasized the need to wait for more data on inflation and the job market before proceeding with further rate cuts, especially given the ongoing concerns about rising prices among businesses and consumers.

Goolsbee was one of three officials who voted against the rate cut in the Fed's 9-3 decision this week, advocating for a delay in rate reductions until early next year. He explained, "Considering that inflation has been above target for four and a half years and progress has stalled in recent months, and given that nearly all businesses and consumers I've spoken with in the district view prices as a major concern, I believe a more cautious approach is to wait for more information."

Goolsbee also mentioned that if future data indicates inflation is returning to the Fed's 2% target, he remains "optimistic" that interest rates could be "significantly lowered" next year.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Goolsbee Optimistic About Potential Rate Cuts Next Year
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve official Goolsbee expressed optimism about the possibility of significant interest rate cuts next year. However, he voiced concerns about the rapid reduction of rates, considering the inflation experienced in recent years.

data.title: Florida prosecutors seized approximately $1.5 million worth of cryptocurrency in an investment fraud case.
data.description: Florida prosecutors have seized approximately $1.5 million worth of cryptocurrency in an investment fraud case. The suspect's wallets reportedly contained cryptocurrencies including AVAX, DOGE, PEPE, SOL, and Bitcoin. The Florida Attorney General's Office's Cyber ​​Fraud Enforcement Division has obtained a court order, and the suspect has been charged with money laundering, grand theft, and organized fraud. (Decrypt)

data.title: Analysts: The Fed is expected to cut rates more than the market anticipates.
data.description: Gold prices continued their upward trend, supported by expectations of further interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve next year and safe-haven demand driven by ongoing geopolitical uncertainty. Analysts at Commerzbank stated, "Although there are signs that the Fed may pause rate cuts at its next meeting in January, the door remains open for further cuts thereafter. We expect the rate cuts to be larger than the market currently anticipates, especially after Powell's successor officially takes office as Fed Chair in May next year." (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitget VIP exclusive USDT investment sold out within 24 hours of its launch; more quotas have now been reopened.
data.description: Bitget launched an exclusive USDT Earn flexible deposit product for VIP users on December 11th, offering up to 10% APR. Reportedly, the product sold out within 24 hours of its launch and subscriptions have since reopened.
